TABLE.NAME: confscheduler
TABLE.COMMENT: Main table for Conference Scheduler activity instances

FIELD.NAME: course
FIELD.COMMENT: Course this activity belongs to

FIELD.NAME: name
FIELD.COMMENT: Activity name

FIELD.NAME: intro
FIELD.COMMENT: Activity description shown on course page

FIELD.NAME: introformat
FIELD.COMMENT: Format of the intro field

FIELD.NAME: confprogramcmid
FIELD.COMMENT: Course-module id of the mod_confprogram instance this scheduler pulls accepted submissions from

FIELD.NAME: conferencestart
FIELD.COMMENT: Organiser-set conference start date/time, unix timestamp; null if not set. Not currently derived from or validated against any scheduled slot -- purely an organiser-declared setting (Revision round 1, 2026-07-03)

FIELD.NAME: conferenceend
FIELD.COMMENT: Organiser-set conference end date/time, unix timestamp; null if not set. See conferencestart's comment

FIELD.NAME: gapminutes
FIELD.COMMENT: GapSnap: minimum gap, in minutes, enforced between presentations in the same room while dragging

FIELD.NAME: pxperhour
FIELD.COMMENT: Row height: vertical pixels per hour of scheduled time in the grid/display timeline, organiser-adjustable in edit mode

FIELD.NAME: notificationsenabled
FIELD.COMMENT: Master switch (user request, 2026-07-06): when 0, this instance's notifier never sends the schedule-change notification, regardless of the template configured or how many slots have a pending change. Defaults to 1 (enabled) so existing instances keep their current behaviour

FIELD.NAME: daystart
FIELD.COMMENT: Daily display-window start, minutes since midnight (e.g. 480 = 08:00). Null means fully automatic (derive the axis from scheduled slots, as before this feature -- user request, 2026-07-06)

FIELD.NAME: dayend
FIELD.COMMENT: Daily display-window end, minutes since midnight. Null means fully automatic, same as daystart

FIELD.NAME: defaultdateview
FIELD.COMMENT: Which day the Display-mode day selector starts on for a first-time/no-preference viewer (user request, 2026-07-07): 'closest' (the day nearest today, previous behaviour) or 'all' (the All days view)

FIELD.NAME: rememberlastday
FIELD.COMMENT: Master switch (user request, 2026-07-07): when 1, a returning user's own last-viewed day (a per-user preference, see classes/api.php::get_last_viewed_day()) takes precedence over defaultdateview above. Defaults to 0 (off) so existing instances keep their current behaviour

TABLE.NAME: confscheduler_room
TABLE.COMMENT: A schedulable room/column within a confscheduler instance

FIELD.NAME: confscheduler
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confscheduler table

FIELD.NAME: name
FIELD.COMMENT: Room name, e.g. Main Hall

FIELD.NAME: sortorder
FIELD.COMMENT: Left-to-right column order in the grid

FIELD.NAME: colour
FIELD.COMMENT: Hex colour (e.g. #3366cc) used to theme this room's column; null means no theme/default styling

FIELD.NAME: capacity
FIELD.COMMENT: Maximum attendee capacity for this room, used only to compute an edit-mode overbooking warning against a scheduled presentation's mod_confprogram favourite count; null means unlimited (never warn)

TABLE.NAME: confscheduler_slot
TABLE.COMMENT: A scheduled block: either a specific presentation, or a column-spanning block (e.g. plenary/lunch) with no presentation

FIELD.NAME: confscheduler
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confscheduler table

FIELD.NAME: submissionid
FIELD.COMMENT: References mod_confsubmissions's confsubmissions_submission.id. Not a real foreign key: it is a cross-plugin reference into another activity's table. Null for a column-spanning block with no presentation

FIELD.NAME: label
FIELD.COMMENT: Used only when submissionid is null, e.g. Lunch Break

FIELD.NAME: colour
FIELD.COMMENT: Hex colour (e.g. #3366cc) used to theme a span block (submissionid null); null means no theme/default styling. Not applicable to presentation slots (Revision round 1, 2026-07-03)

FIELD.NAME: notifiedtime
FIELD.COMMENT: Timestamp a schedule-change notification was last sent for this slot's current scheduling info, or 0 if never sent. Pending (needs (re-)notifying) whenever this is 0 or less than timemodified. Only meaningful for presentation slots (submissionid non-null); span blocks are never notified. Sending is manually triggered via the edit-mode 'Send notifications' button, never automatic (user request, 2026-07-05)

TABLE.NAME: confscheduler_slotroom
TABLE.COMMENT: Junction table: the room(s) a slot occupies. A normal single-room presentation has exactly one row; a column-spanning block has one row per room it spans

FIELD.NAME: slotid
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confscheduler_slot table

FIELD.NAME: roomid
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confscheduler_room table

TABLE.NAME: confscheduler_notiftemplate
TABLE.COMMENT: Organiser-edited notification template (email subject/body) for this instance's schedule-change notification

FIELD.NAME: confscheduler
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confscheduler table

FIELD.NAME: notiftype
FIELD.COMMENT: Always 'scheduled' currently (a single notification type) -- kept as its own column rather than a hardcoded single row per instance so a second notification type can be added later without a schema change

FIELD.NAME: subject
FIELD.COMMENT: Notification email subject, with embeddable placeholder fields (e.g. [[fullname]], [[submissiontitle]]); null/blank falls back to a built-in default

FIELD.NAME: body
FIELD.COMMENT: Notification email body, same placeholder syntax as subject; null/blank falls back to a built-in default

FIELD.NAME: bodyformat
FIELD.COMMENT: Standard Moodle text-format field for the body column

TABLE.NAME: confscheduler_daybounds
TABLE.COMMENT: Per-conference-day override of the daily display-window start/end. A day without a row here inherits the instance-level confscheduler.daystart/dayend default. Keyed by a Y-m-d day key (the same day identity the grid's client-side code uses) rather than a timestamp, to avoid the browser-vs-server timezone ambiguity documented in amd/src/day_utils.js

FIELD.NAME: confscheduler
FIELD.COMMENT: Foreign key to confscheduler table

FIELD.NAME: day
FIELD.COMMENT: The conference day this window applies to, as a Y-m-d day key (e.g. 2026-07-10)

FIELD.NAME: daystart
FIELD.COMMENT: Display-window start for this day, minutes since midnight (e.g. 480 = 08:00)

FIELD.NAME: dayend
FIELD.COMMENT: Display-window end for this day, minutes since midnight; always strictly greater than daystart
